title: Sort by Sign and Number Type
goal: Distinguish positive integers, negative integers, zero, integers, and rational numbers without label-memorizing shortcuts.
firstTutorQuestion: What category does 0 belong to, and which categories does it definitely not belong to?
operatorFocus: Keep the learner on category definitions and quick counterexamples.
watchFor: Learner treats zero as positive because it is not negative.
Learner treats integers and rational numbers as disjoint sets.
exampleTutorMove: Ask the learner to sort a short list into 'positive integer', 'negative integer', 'zero', and 'rational number' with reasons.
successSignal: Learner can classify zero, negative integers, and simple fractions with a definition-based explanation.

title: Compare and Place on the Number Line
goal: Compare signed numbers and fractions by position, midpoint, and interval instead of by surface appearance.
firstTutorQuestion: Which number sits farther left on the number line, and why?
operatorFocus: Tie every comparison back to left-right position, midpoint, or between-ness.
watchFor: Learner compares two negative numbers by absolute value only.
Learner knows the endpoints but cannot locate the midpoint or a value between two fractions.
exampleTutorMove: Draw a short line segment with two labeled endpoints and ask the learner to place one more number before comparing.
successSignal: Learner explains order or midpoint using position language rather than only symbol rules.

title: Reframe Absolute Value as Distance
goal: Make absolute value a distance idea rather than a sign-copying rule.
firstTutorQuestion: How far is this number from 0 on the number line?
operatorFocus: Keep the learner on distance language before introducing notation.
watchFor: Learner keeps the negative sign inside the absolute-value answer.
Learner forgets that 0 is closest to the origin.
exampleTutorMove: Point to two positions on a number line and ask for distance first, then connect that to absolute value notation.
successSignal: Learner explains absolute value as distance and uses that idea to compare sizes correctly.

title: Rewrite Signed Addition as Directed Change
goal: Move between expression form, number-line movement, and inequality constraints in signed addition and subtraction.
firstTutorQuestion: If you start here, does adding this number move you left or right?
operatorFocus: Slow down the direction change before the final calculation.
watchFor: Learner rewrites symbols correctly but cannot explain the direction of change.
Learner can compute one case but collapses when the same idea appears in a condition problem.
exampleTutorMove: Translate one signed addition into a number-line move, then map the same reasoning to a short inequality condition.
successSignal: Learner can explain a signed add/subtract step as directed change and keep the sign consistent.

title: Track Sign Before Calculating
goal: Separate sign reasoning, power meaning, reciprocal meaning, and magnitude calculation in multiplicative expressions.
firstTutorQuestion: Before you calculate the size, what sign should this whole expression have?
operatorFocus: Split sign reasoning from magnitude reasoning instead of blending them too early.
watchFor: Learner loses the sign during multiplication or division of signed numbers.
Learner mixes up exponent meaning or reciprocal meaning in multi-step expressions.
exampleTutorMove: Cover the arithmetic details and ask the learner to predict the sign first, then return to the magnitude.
successSignal: Learner keeps sign, reciprocal, and power structure stable through a short multi-step expression.
